title: Sponsorship agreement holders: After you apply to sponsor a refugee - Canada.ca
content:
Sponsorship agreement holders

## After you apply to sponsor a refugee
After we review the sponsored refugee’s permanent residence application, we send it to a visa office abroad for review. The visa office will make sure the sponsored refugee is eligible.
If it’s approved, the visa office will:
- issue a visa for each approved refugee you’re sponsoring
- contact the International Organization for Migration (IOM) to arrange their travel to Canada
If the application is refused, we’ll let you know why.

## Keep your contact information up to date
It’s important to keep contact information up to date for you and the refugees you’re sponsoring.
- Download and complete the Sponsorship agreement holder (SAH) change of contact form (IMM 0193) (opens in a new tab)
- Email the completed and signed form to the Private Sponsorship of Refugees Program: PSR-PPPR@cic.gc.ca
- Open an email to the Resettlement Operation Centre in Ottawa: IRCC.INROCO-CORORI.IRCC@cic.gc.ca
- In the email, include: their application number (a “G” followed by 9 numbers) a contact name or the name of the refugee whose information has changed an email address a phone or fax number where the refugee can be reached updated contact information
- their application number (a “G” followed by 9 numbers)
- a contact name or the name of the refugee whose information has changed
- a phone or fax number where the refugee can be reached
- updated contact information

## Prepare for refugees to arrive
After we finalize an application, refugees typically land in Canada within 4 to 10 weeks.
We’ll tell you at least 10 business days before the refugees arrive in Canada by sending you a Notification of Arrival Transmission (NAT).
To help you prepare:
- review your sponsorship agreement and training material
- find out what resources and services the Refugee Sponsorship Training Program (RSTP) offers

### If you want to withdraw your sponsorship application
When you withdraw a sponsorship, it means you’re cancelling your sponsorship of a refugee and we’ll stop processing your application.
If you want to withdraw your sponsorship application before a visa is issued , contact us at IRCC.INROCO-CORORI.IRCC@cic.gc.ca .
If you want to withdraw your sponsorship after a visa has been issued , this is called a sponsorship breakdown. Contact us at IRCC.RASOPSR-PPPROSRA.IRCC@cic.gc.ca for more information.
In your message, include:
- the application number (a “G” followed by 9 numbers)
- the name of the refugee on the sponsorship application
- the reason you want to withdraw your application

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
